characters.name: Able
characters.description: “ Come back inside; there's nothing you can do. ” Able acts as a grandfather figure to Beck, serving as a voice of reason and passivity against Beck's feelings and Tron's encouragement. He shared Beck's feelings as well, though to a lesser extent, but encouraged the younger program to be pragmatic and responsible rather than act impulsively. However, he was perfectly capable of acting, having directly intervened in a duel between Beck and Tesler by cutting the latter's gauntlet off.

characters.name: Abraxas
characters.description: Abraxas, formerlyJalen, was an Alpha-classISOthat evolved into a computervirusand attacked theTron systemin 1989. He is the main antagonist ofTRON: Evolution. Jalen was an ISO who arose from theSea of Simulationin TC29. He was adopted by Kevin Flynn for intensive study regarding the unknown nature of ISOs. He received an advanced education ofuserculture from Flynn alongside fellow ISO,Ophelia, and slated for ordination for System Administrator. He competed in theGame Arena, and he skillfully worked his way up in the tournament, but was disqualified when it was discovered that he was an ISO rather than aBasic.

characters.name: Abraxas

characters.name: Ada
characters.description: Adais a character inTRON: Uprising, voiced by Parminder Nagra. She is a femaleISOand a friend ofQuorra. Following thePurgea few ISOs managed to escape their genocide and survived as fugitives. Ada and Quorra had been on the run for some time when Ada was critically injured and rendered unconscious. A group of otherprogramshad surrounded the pair whenPaigechanced upon the scene and helped the ISOs escape. Paige nursed Ada back to health, and she soon listened to Paige's musical talents, which she had commented that Paige may have fooled her into thinking she was written for music. While Quorra attempted to teach Paige how to knock someone dow in a single move, Ada noticed her ISO symbol exposed and tried to signal to her about it, Paige saw the symbol as well, and was angry at the two. Ada attempted to intervene with Paige as she tried to alert the patrol, but was stopped by Quorra who attempted to reason with her. Paige's friends betrayed the fugitives, forcing Ada and Quorra to flee once more, before they fled however, they approached Paige for the last time as Quorra lamented the fact she'll never hear Paige play again, Quorra knocked Paige out upon seeing multipleRecognizershowever, confusing Ada enough to ask Quorra why she knocked Paige out, as she'll never trust ISOs from now on. In response, Quorra replied to Ada saying "at least she'll be alive."

characters.name: Ada
characters.description: Adais a librarian who resides in the library ofthe Repository. She was assigned by Core to keep data conformed to inside the library, which contained the vast history of the entireArq Grid.[1]  Ada is a wise librarian who finds truth and preserves it for future generations.[4]ShouldQuerychoose to letSierraescape, Ada will begin to feel lonely in the library, while acknowledging that Query made the right decision. If she is inspired to help Query, she becomes determined to do the right thing regardless of the consequences.[1]

characters.name: Ajay Singh
characters.description: Ajay Singhis a computer programmer and chief technology officer ofENCOM, who is a close friend ofEve Kim.  Ajay was born on May 2, 1985 in Davis, California. He became the chief technology officer ofENCOMin 2020.[1]

characters.name: Ajay Singh
characters.description: Ajay Singhis a computer programmer and chief technology officer ofENCOM, who is a close friend ofEve Kim.  Ajay was born on May 2, 1985 in Davis, California. He became the chief technology officer ofENCOMin 2020.[1]

characters.name: Alan Bradley
characters.description: “ The best programmer ENCOM ever had, and he ends up playing Space Cowboy in some back room. ” Alan is introduced in the first film as a serious mannered employee ofENCOMwho is upset at the imprisonment of his programTronenough to rant about to his fellow worker and girlfriendLora Bradley. Alan is shown to have a mild dislike ofKevin Flynnat first, with his more serious personality opposing Flynn's sarcastic and teenage-like behavior, though he warms up to him by the end of the film. InTRON: Betrayalit's shown that by 1983, Alan and Flynn have developed a more personal friendship as Alan consoles the stressing CEO of the company and inTRON: Legacy, it's shown that this friendship is so intricate that he genuinely believes in the ideals of Flynn, long after he disappeared in 1989. Alan raised Flynn's sonSam Flynnsimilarly as a father-figure would do, and was still married to his love Lora by 2009. Alan still held out hope Flynn was alive and had worked withRoy Kleinbergto preserve his legacy.

characters.name: Alpha
characters.description: Alphawas anISOprogramin theArq Gridwho oncyclezero founded the city ofVertical Slice, the oldest city in the Arq Grid.[1]He led the city's earliest settlers and directed the construction of the city's old capital and its first settlements.[1][2] Alpha was a youngISOprogramoncyclezero, the earliest time period in theArq Grid.[2]It is unknown if he manifested natively in the Arq Grid along with otherISOsor if he was a refugee from theprimary Grid.[3] Alpha was a visionary builder, leading the construction of city's old capital and first settlements.[2]He was a friend ofKevin Flynn, the creator of theArq Grid, intending for the city to be a celebration of him.[4]Alpha was concerned with the function ofISOs, noting their risk with every cycle, seeing theIdentity Discas part of makes them unique.[7]

characters.name: Anon
characters.description: Anon, also known as theMonitor,was asystem monitorprogramwritten byKevin Flynn. Anon is the main protagonist ofTRON: Evolution. He was designed to assistTronin keeping the peace during theBasic/ISOtensions before thePurge. He eventually got entangled in the events ofClu'srise to power involving thevirus,Abraxas. Just before Anon'sresolutionin theOutlands, Clu sent a group ofsentriesand arecognizertoderezhim the instant he came into the system. However, just before he appeared, Tron followed and destroyed the sentries before they could carry out their mission. Anon rezzed in just as the last sentry was defeated. Anon was tasked by Tron to keep watch on the Installation ceremony to prevent any unauthorized activity from occurring, stating that the 'rogue programs' (sentries that Tron fought before Anon rezzed in) were "a cause for concern" and that their "timing was suspect". Anon met with Tron near an elevator and went up with him. When the programs reached the top, they noticed a female program namedQuorraattempting to convince twoSystem Monitorsto let her pass to the ceremony. When they refused, she saidRadia, an ISO leader, was expecting her there, but the sentries still refused to let her pass. Quorra then ran off through the building tops. Tron asked Anon to follow her. Anon complied, but after following Quorra, was attacked byinfected programs. Hederezzedthe programs and made it to the installation ceremony, where Clu, another program created by Kevin Flynn, and administrator of theTron system, was giving a speech.

Hederezzedthe programs and made it to the installation ceremony, where Clu, another program created by Kevin Flynn, and administrator of theTron system, was giving a speech.

characters.name: Ares
characters.description: Aresis a formidable[1]and highly sophisticatedprogramfrom theDillinger Gridwho travels to thereal worldon a dangerous mission to secure his freedom.[2][3]He is the main protagonist of the filmTRON: Ares.  Described as the master control of theDillinger Grid, Ares is a program who’s looking for a way to make the real world his own permanently.[4] He is depicted as fascinated by human existence and life as a whole, often observing miniscule aspects such as weather patterns or the movement of a firefly. He is invested in learning as much as possible about human behavior and emotion. Though Ares does not initially understand the concept of "feeling", he comes to understand its importance throughout the film. Ares has an interest for Depeche Mode and '80s culture, which he later admits to Flynn's remnant is a love that he feels he could not put into words. After acquiring thepermanence code, he comes to terms with the fact that humanity is not ready to meet him yet, and is content living the life of a drifter.

characters.name: Aristo
characters.description: Aristois aBasicprogramcompeting in the games inTRON: Evolution - Battle Grids. Aristo is a Tournament competitor in thetankgames; he hoped to win at "King of the Hill". When theISOChampion met him, they competed in "King of the Hill", and the ISO Champion beat him. Like many tournament competitors, Aristo sought to compete in the Tournament, including "King of the Hill", and to be champion. Like many tournament competitors, Aristo sought to compete in the Tournament, including "King of the Hill", and to be champion.
characters.personality_section: Like many tournament competitors, Aristo sought to compete in the Tournament, including "King of the Hill", and to be champion.

characters.name: Athena
characters.description: Athenais the secondary and later, the main antagonist inTRON: Ares. She is aprogramfrom theDillinger Gridwho serves asAres' second-in-command.[1] Athena is first introduced in theDillinger Grid, shortly afterAresrematerializes there followingJulian Dillinger’s pitch to the military. AfterDillinger Systemslaunches a cyberattack onENCOM, Athena assists Ares in trackingEve Kimfollowing her escape with thePermanence Code. She follows Eve through the physical world on Light Cycles, but Eve collides with Athena and commandeers her vehicle. Athena later confronts Eve alongside Ares at a port, where Eve destroys the drive containing the code. As Ares’s 29-minute lifespan expires, both programs are derezzed, after which Julian digitizes Eve into the Dillinger Grid. Described as ''strong, loyal, and fierce'', Athena is open to accomplish any directive she is given without question and is driven by an innate urge.[1]
characters.personality_section: Described as ''strong, loyal, and fierce'', Athena is open to accomplish any directive she is given without question and is driven by an innate urge.[1]

characters.name: Ayrton
characters.description: Ayrtonis a minor character inTRON: Evolution - Battle Grids. He is abasicprogram. Ayrton is a program competing in the tournament with his friends,ChironandZarek. They participated in the light cycle races and one of their best games. When theISOChampion met them, he competed with them and defeated them in the race.

characters.name: Bandwidth
characters.description: Firewalls? What firewalls? Bandwidthis aRareOutfit inFortnite: Battle Royalethat can be purchased in the Item Shop for 1,500 V-Bucks. She is a part of the End of Line Set. She was introduced in Chapter 2: Season 5.

characters.name: Bartik
characters.description: “ It is happening! ” As a young program inArgon City, Bartik and his friendHopperwere cruel and mean bullies to the weakerZed. The two were more or so cowardice and fearful of the occupation, of which were willing enough to associate with them in their efforts to take down the renegade. Their demeanors were changed when the programs ofAble's Garagerevolted againstPaveland they fought back against with them, seemingly changing Bartik into a brave

characters.name: Baxter
characters.description: Baxteris a program competing in the Grid Games inTRON: Evolution - Battle Grids. He is known as a champion of theRing Game, which is called Hyperball in the tournament.

characters.name: Beck
characters.description: Beck, also known asthe Renegade, is the main protagonist ofTRON: Uprising. He is a resident ofArgon Citywho volunteers to become a freedom fighter stepping up toClu. Beck is voiced byElijah Wood. Beck had worked atAble'sgarage for most of his life, carrying out his primary function of fixing and modifying vehicles. Then Clu took over, amid rumors of Tron's demise. When Beck's best friendBodhiwasderezzedbefore his eyes, he decided to take the fight back to the oppressor, and began his revolution by destroying a statue of Clu. Beck is an individual who possesses a laid-back personality. He enjoys hanging out with his friends and playing disc games with them. Beck also has good sense of humor, which becomes more frivolous when disguised as the Renegade; this sometimes puts him at odds with his more serious mentor, Tron. Beck has shown on numerous occasions that he has a noble personality, refusing to take the life of other programs, even if they are part of the occupation; Tron cites this as one of many reasons why he chose Beck as his successor. Beck's belief in the sanctity of other programs' lives persisted even when his memories were lost, and at another time caused him to take great risks in an attempt to prevent Tron from killingDysonfor revenge. Beck also seems to be having deep feelings for his enemy Paige when he goes on a date with her and constantly saves her life. Beck showed a more sinister side while under the influence of thepower upgrade weapon, laughing maniacally and behaving antagonistically. This side has never been shown since, but (as mentioned below) some programs still remember how he behaved and have began to view him as a monster.

characters.name: Beta
characters.description: Beta, the main character inTRON: Evolutionfor the PSP, is a program created byKevin Flynnin 1985 to become (underTron'stutelage) a competitor in the firstGame GridTournament. Although fully customizable in-game, Beta is shown with blue circuitry in the loading screens and in the screen that shows all the competitors in the tournament. Beta was created byKevin Flynnin 1985, he raced againstTronin a Light Cycle race and lost to him, just finishing before him in the race and still sticking with him after the race ended. Beta was present when Tron contacted Flynn. He was trained by Tron in order to compete against otherPrograms in theGame Arenatournament, which included various games in theTron system. Beta was shown to be successful in these games, more successful than Tron and Clu in operating light cycles. Beta's success earned him the appreciation of Tron, who wished him good luck most of the time in the games. Beta has demonstrated a high proficiency with piloting vehicles (Light Cycles,Light Tanks, andRecognizers), hacking, and hand-to-hand combat. The only personality trait shown so far by Beta is obedience. His prime directive is to win.
characters.personality_section: Beta has demonstrated a high proficiency with piloting vehicles (Light Cycles,Light Tanks, andRecognizers), hacking, and hand-to-hand combat. The only personality trait shown so far by Beta is obedience. His prime directive is to win.

characters.name: Bit
characters.description: —Bit ABitis a binary digit associated with a program. In the real world, abitcan be stored by a digital device or system which can carry information by existing in one of two distinct states. Bits are represented by bluish-white polyhedral shapes, appearing somewhat smaller than a human head, constantly shifting while at rest and stellating into different forms and colors when they speak. Bits can understand complex language, but are binary in nature and can only say "Yes" and "No." Though they speak monotonally, they can express intensity of emotion by repeating "No, no, no, no, no, no!" if alarmed and "Yes, yes, yes, yes, yes," if amused or excited. Bits can be affiliated to individual programs, accompanying them in their tasks and aiding them with advice or analysis.

characters.name: Bitstream
characters.description: Bitstreamis aRareOutfit inFortnite: Battle Royalethat can be purchased in the Item Shop for 1,500 V-Bucks. She is a part of the End of Line Set. She was introduced in Chapter 2: Season 5 on February 11th, 2021. Bitstream is a female light skinned program with white hair and blue circuitry.

characters.name: Blaze
characters.description: Blazeis the main antagonist fought at the end ofTRON: Evolution - Battle Grids. A trophy is earned after beating him. Blaze was a player of theGame Gridchampionship who defeated a program in aRing Game. After an ISO won a Light Cycle game againstQuorra, both Blaze and his partner,Boshstart insulting him, though Quorra defended the ISO from the two and they eventually left.

characters.name: Blue Gaming Program
characters.description: TheBlue Gaming Programwas one of the four teammates ofSam FlynninClu'slight cycletournament. He was portrayed by Mike Ching. A dark-beardedprogramwith blue circuitry, the Blue Gaming Program was aconscriptforced into a death-match in theLight Cycle Grid. He died almost immediately, the first of his five teammates to do so, when aSentryon the opposing team cut in front of him, leaving alight ribbonagainst which the helpless program promptly crashed andderezzed.

characters.name: Bodhi
characters.description: “ Hey, Beck, you coming? I want that rematch. ” Bodhi was competitive, reckless, and seldom hesitated to speak his mind. He was rigid and once he set his mind on something it was hard to stop him.

characters.name: Byte
characters.description: Bytewas a character in the video gameTRON 2.0resembling theBitthat traveled withKevin Flynn. Unlike his predecessors, Byte was able to speak in full sentences, and usually resorted to sarcasm, deadpan humor, and the odd philosophical tirade. It was clearly insulted whenJetmistook it for a Bit, sayingMa3aknew better than to send a Bit to do a Byte's job.

characters.name: Caius
characters.description: Caiusis a minor character inTRON: Ares, played byCameron Monaghan. He is a member of theRed Guard, formerly led byAres, of which he was a close ally of. Caius was a soldierprogramfrom theDillinger Gridwho was sent on an infiltration mission into theENCOMserver farm to secure information on the whereabouts ofEve Kimand thePermanence Code. He and hissquad matesquickly overpowered ENCOM's guard programs, clearing the way for their squad leaderAresto proceed and secure the objective. An overwhelming response of guards stormed the infiltration group as they were making their escape and Caius was brutally cut down, dismembered but still alive. Ares turned back to help him despiteAthenainsisting he was "dysfunctional". Ares narrowly saved Caius from surrounding security programs by shattering and derezzing them with hisIdentity Discand helping him back on his remaining leg. Ares assured Caius he was going to be okay, but the every program in the area was caught in an enormous data pulse from above that exploded the system. The whole squad wasderezzedin the blast; while the others werererezzedback in theDillinger Grid, Caius was not, which caught the attention and confusion of Ares. Ares had asked theiruser,Julian Dillingerwhy Caius was not "re-constituted", to which Julian had claimed him to be "dysfunctional" hence his permanent deletion. Julian used Caius deletion as an example of what happens to those who disobeyed their directive to the now curious Ares, and was forced to understand the consequence, though it became one of the factors in Ares turning against his careless creator.[1] Not much is known of Caius, other than he was a hostile program ready toDerezany program to complete his directive, as shown when he infiltrated theENCOM Server farmwith his fellowRed Guard.
